steps.text: Peer review the radiochemical method of analysis.
Publish the radiochemical method of analysis.
Determine if a method is a good candidate for publication as an EPA method.
Pursue formal validation phases.

preconditions.text: Substantial evidence exists that a method is a good candidate for publication as an EPA method.

constraints.text: Proper planning is critical for successful method validation studies.

steps.text: Initiate an intralaboratory method validation study.
Repeat some or all of the performance tests.
Initiate an interlaboratory method validation study.
Repeat some or all aspects of the interlaboratory study.
Clearly describe the components of a method or measurement process requiring validation.
Evaluate all process components combined during validation efforts.
Clearly and completely state the measurement-process components validated and the combination of procedures composing a method.
Clearly define the purpose of a method and the intended use of the data.
Well define and clearly describe method scope and applicability.

preconditions.text: A detailed procedure has been written, operational parameters are believed to be optimized and finalized, and no additional changes are anticipated.
Procedural changes are made that may affect the method performance characteristics.
A successful intralaboratory study has been completed.
Procedural changes are required after initiating a multiple laboratory study.

constraints.text: Method scope and applicability must include: measurement process components validated, nature of analytes and matrices studied, range of analyte levels, description of known limitations/assumptions, and description of how parameters meet DQOs/MQOs.

steps.text: Investigate the stability of the analyte(s) from the time of sampling through the time of analysis.
Investigate the extract stability.
Validate the analytical method.
Calculate a combined standard uncertainty by propagating the standard uncertainties of the measured values used to calculate the result.
Multiply the combined standard uncertainty by a coverage factor to obtain an expanded uncertainty.

preconditions.text: Ensure that proper sample preservation and storage stability studies were performed during method development.

constraints.text: If the analysis is based on the molecular form that the radionuclide is in, special precautions must address the chemical stability of that molecule and not necessarily the oxidation state of the radionuclide.
The chemical state of a radionuclide may be changed through oxidation-reduction reactions within the matrix if not properly preserved.

steps.text: Estimate method bias by measuring reference materials, comparing results to at least one alternate method or procedure, or analyzing spiked materials.
Report the estimated bias with an uncertainty.

constraints.text: Bias can arise if the measurement method is improperly calibrated or calibrated with reference materials not traceable to the national standard.
Bias can arise if the measurement method fails to quantitatively measure all of the radionuclide present.
Bias can arise if the measurement method fails to correct properly for the chemical yield of the radionuclide or element.
Bias can arise if the measurement method fails to correct properly for the contribution of instrument background or reagent activity to the sample result.
Bias can arise if the presence of another radionuclide interferes with the determination of the analyte.

steps.text: Evaluate trueness at the extremes of the quantitation range stated in the scope of a method, and at regulatory levels.

constraints.text: The reference materials approach introduces the important uncertainty of matrix matching, and the reliability of a bias estimate depends upon the relationship between the composition of the reference material and the samples.
When using the alternate method approach, the reliability of a bias estimate is dependent upon how much is known about the performance characteristics of the alternate method.

The matrix spiking approach introduces uncertainty regarding the behavior of spiked materials, compared to materials containing native analyte, which may be particularly problematic for solid materials.

steps.text: Verify the estimated MDC by testing materials containing analyte at the claimed detection level.
Evaluate method trueness and precision at the extremes of the analyte concentration range.
Evaluate method specificity by testing appropriate matrix blanks and fortified (spiked) matrix blanks.
Test a worst-case sample matrix during both intra- and interlaboratory validation studies.
Study actual samples of material containing an undetermined amount of the inherent radionuclide to evaluate method selectivity.
Use multiple analytical procedures during an intralaboratory validation study to substantiate the specificity of the method being evaluated.
Use a combination of procedures or techniques that are based on different measurement principles.

preconditions.text: Adequate representations of matrix blanks cannot be obtained or prepared.

constraints.text: Matrix blanks should contain the chemical species and inherent radionuclides (e.g., naturally occurring radionuclides), other than the radionuclide(s) of interest, which are reasonably expected to be present in an actual sample.
An analyte concentration range must include either a regulatory limit or a defined action level, typically near the midpoint of the range of radionuclide activity level (concentration) for a project.

steps.text: Conduct ruggedness testing
Identify critical control points in the documented method
Initiate an interlaboratory method validation study
Publish an EPA method for general use

preconditions.text: Ruggedness testing must be completed before initiating an interlaboratory method validation study
Critical control points must be identified in the documented method before initiating an interlaboratory method validation study
An interlaboratory method validation study must be completed prior to publishing an EPA method for general use

constraints.text: The fractional factorial experimental design is not intended for method optimization purposes.

steps.text: Document any deviations that occur from the interlaboratory study protocol.
Prepare a method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.

preconditions.text: A successful intralaboratory study has been completed and the issuing entity has demonstrated a pressing programmatic need for publishing a national method.

constraints.text: Deviations from the interlaboratory study protocol should be strongly discouraged.

The report should address the method validation topics outlined in Table 1 (Purpose, Scope and Applicability, Method Specificity, Method Uncertainty), background information on method development, a description of the actual method validation techniques, any changes made to the method as a result of the validation studies, and any recommendations for future work.

steps.text: Evaluate trueness using samples that span the degree of analytical difficulty encompassed by the specified scope and applicability.
Evaluate the method for both repeatability (within-lab) and reproducibility (among labs).
Verify MQC estimates by analyzing samples containing radionuclide at the claimed MQC level.
Verify MDC estimates by analyzing samples containing radionuclide at the claimed detection level.
Characterize analytical measurement uncertainty over an analyte concentration range.
Evaluate method ruggedness during method validation.
Include the design and results of the interlaboratory study in the method validation summary report.
Report data from the interlaboratory study in tabular form.
Include a discussion describing the details of, and rationale for, any changes made to the method resulting from the interlaboratory study.

preconditions.text: The method is designed for demonstrating compliance with regulatory requirements and evaluation is practical.
Evaluation of method ruggedness is practical.

steps.text: Peer review EPA methods of analysis in accordance with the most current version of the EPA Science Policy Council’s Peer Review Handbook.

preconditions.text: Before publication of EPA methods of analysis
